data.narrative:
Iambic Therapeutics is a Series B-stage biotech company that has raised $220M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 5 private peers at identical stage (Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, Profluent) and 3 public sector benchmarks (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L), all within Biotech & Health.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $220M raised proxy, yielding $418M. The peer-median approach across the 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $810M; this peer set ranges from $600M (Profluent) to $3.3B (Eikon Therapeutics), with a midpoint of $810M. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (5.8x median across the 3 public tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a valuation range of $355M (low), $614M (median), and $931M (high). The peer-median method anchors toward the upper bound given the substantial dataset; stage-progression provides more conservative grounding. Key uncertainties include stage-to-stage progression timing, differentiation relative to the $600M–$3.3B peer dispersion, and revenue trajectory post-Series B.
